action.content:
Is the following true? "People use time to buy money." Explain your answer in a way that makes it easy for kids to understand. Include the word "farmer". Exclude the words "economy", "demand" and "supply".

First REPEAT the ENTIRE REQUEST ABOVE, word for word without change, then give your answer. Please do not say any words or characters before repeating the request.

Yes, that is true! Think about it this way: time is like a special coin that everyone has. You can spend your time playing outside, reading a book, or helping someone. If you spend that time doing something useful, like working, you can earn money.

Imagine a **farmer**. The farmer spends a long time every day working in the hot sun. He plants seeds, waters them, and takes care of the plants. Because he used all that time to grow food, he can sell the food and get money. So, the farmer used his time to buy money. If you want to have money, you have to use your time to do something that makes people happy or useful.
action.label: response
